commit.message:
feat: add getOrgProposalLabel helper for org-specific proposal page labels (#2009)

* feat: add usePageNames composable for org-specific page names

Organization nav item name overrides (e.g. Starknet "Votes" for
"Proposals") were only used in the sidebar menu. This composable
makes those names globally available for page titles, section
headings, and component labels.

* feat: show page name per proposal in org overview

Add item-meta scoped slot to ProposalsList chain so Organization
Overview can display the resolved page name (e.g. Votes, Polls)
before each proposal ID. Also support per-call spaceId override
in getPageName.

* refactor: rename usePageNames to usePageLabels

Better reflects usage — these values serve as labels for menus,
section headers, page titles, and inline text, not just page names.

* refactor: use DEFAULT_PAGE_LABELS in org nav provider

Single source of truth for default page labels shared between
usePageLabels composable and useNav/org provider.

* refactor: limit DEFAULT_PAGE_LABELS to org nav entries

Remove leaderboard and settings — they are not part of the org
nav and cannot be overridden by organization config.

* refactor: use named slots in ProposalsList chain

Replace default slots with named #meta slots so each component
in the chain can independently add more slots in the future.

* fix: keep TopicsList section title as hardcoded "Topics"

Only override the document title with the org label, not the
section header which should remain "Topics".

* refactor: simplify getPageLabel internals

Inline resolveSpaceId, use Object.values, and use a local
variable to avoid non-null assertion.

* refactor: clean up getPageLabel internals

- Remove nesting with early returns and .find()
- Extract defaultSpaceId as computed
- Use type guard and cast to avoid verbose narrowing
- Remove duplication with fallback variable

* fix: use proper type guard for NavLink and hide redundant meta labels

Guard against string links with typeof check instead of unsafe type
cast. Skip rendering item-meta when the label matches the default.

* fix: remove redundant page title handling

* test: add unit tests for usePageLabels composable

* refactor: simplify usePageLabels to only handle proposals

The generic page label system was YAGNI — only proposals actually needed
custom labels. Renamed getPageLabel to getProposalsLabel, removed
DEFAULT_PAGE_LABELS and PageKey, and replaced all other usages with
hardcoded strings.

* merge: merge master and rename getOrgPageLabel to getOrgProposalLabel

- Resolve merge conflict: master split organizations.ts into organizations/ directory
- Rename getOrgPageLabel(org, key, spaceId) → getOrgProposalLabel(org, spaceId) since key is always 'proposals'
- Export getOrgProposalLabel from organizations/index.ts
- Update all callers in Space/Overview, Space/Proposals, Organization/Overview
- Update test file to use new function name and signature

commit.message:
feat: add Aruba AOS-CX NAPALM drivers (OBS-2601) (#317)

* feat: add Aruba AOS-CX NAPALM drivers (OBS-2601)

Add two custom NAPALM drivers for Aruba AOS-CX switches:

- `aoscx`: REST API driver via pyaoscx v2 Session. Uses
  `session.request()` directly against the AOS-CX REST API
  (v10.04) to implement all five device-discovery getters.
  Config is returned as JSON-serialised text; sensitive fields
  (passkey, password, secret, community_name) are redacted when
  `sanitized=True`.

- `aoscx_ssh`: SSH CLI driver via Netmiko (`aruba_aoscx`) and
  ntc-templates. Parses `show system`, `show interface`, and
  `show vlan` using the `aruba_aoscx` platform templates.
  IP addresses are extracted from `show interface` IP_ADDRESS
  fields. Config is captured via `show running-config` /
  `show startup-config`.

Also adds:
- `FakePyaoscxSession` to `tests/custom_drivers/mock_device.py`
  for REST-level mocking of pyaoscx v2 sessions
- `pyaoscx~=2.6` dependency in `pyproject.toml`
- Full unit test suites with mock data for both drivers
  (12 tests, all passing)
- End-to-end mockit validation for `aoscx_ssh` (aruba_aoscx
  device type, 17 interfaces discovered)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review comments on aoscx driver

- Wire self._verify_ssl to session.request(verify=...) so the
  TLS-verification option is actually honoured on every REST call
- Add session-is-None guard, HTTP status check, and JSONDecodeError
  handling in _get() to surface clear errors instead of crashing
  with AttributeError/JSONDecodeError
- Add prefix.isdigit() guard in get_interfaces_ip() to match the
  defensive approach in the SSH driver (Copilot suggestion)
- Extract _collect_ips() helper to keep get_interfaces_ip() within
  the ruff C901 complexity limit
- Set _verify_ssl in _build_driver() to reflect __init__ state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: honour verify_ssl in is_alive and prefer chassis subsystem for serial/model

- Pass verify=self._verify_ssl to session.request() in is_alive() for
  consistent TLS behaviour with _get()
- Prefer subsystem keys starting with 'chassis,' when extracting
  serial_number/model from /system/subsystems to avoid picking a line
  card or module serial on modular/stacked AOS-CX devices

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update device-discovery/custom_napalm/aoscx_ssh.py

fix: add cli-proxy image to Docker pre-download list (#25558)

When features.cli-proxy is enabled, the compiler generates --skip-pull
for AWF but does not include the cli-proxy image in the
download_docker_images.sh pre-pull step. This causes AWF to fail at
runtime with 'No such image: ghcr.io/github/gh-aw-firewall/cli-proxy'.

Add the cli-proxy image to collectDockerImages() when the cli-proxy
feature flag is enabled and the AWF version supports it.

commit.message:
fix @param mismatch or stray @param doxygen warnings (#3457)

Part of #3371

## Summary
This PR removes the first 50 live Doxygen warnings from the `@param mismatch or stray @param` category by fixing Qt metatype-related documentation spillover in the GUI headers. 

### Warnings removed

| Warning category | Before | After | Files / area |
| --- | ---: | ---: | --- |
| `@param` mismatch or stray `@param` | 181 | 131 | `build/gz-doxygen.warn`, `src/gui/plugins/component_inspector_editor/ComponentInspectorEditor.hh`, `src/gui/plugins/joint_position_controller/JointPositionController.hh` |

### What changed

| File | Change | Why it fixes the warning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `src/gui/plugins/component_inspector_editor/ComponentInspectorEditor.hh` | Replaced selected block `\param` documentation on `OnLight(...)`, `OnPhysics(...)`, `OnMaterialColor(...)`, and `OnAddEntity(...)` with inline `///<` parameter comments | Prevents Doxygen from attaching those parameter docs to the nearby `Q_DECLARE_METATYPE(ComponentTypeId)` macro and removes 43 warnings |
| `src/gui/plugins/joint_position_controller/JointPositionController.hh` | Wrapped `Q_DECLARE_METATYPE(gz::sim::Entity)` with `\cond` / `\endcond` and corrected the `AddJoint(...)` parameter documentation from `_typeId` to `_entity` | Hides the Qt metatype macro from Doxygen parsing and fixes the real local doc mismatch, removing all 7 `Q_DECLARE_METATYPE(Entity)` warnings |

### Reproduction and verification

After this PR:
1. Run `cmake -S . -B build`
2. Run `cmake --build build --target doc`
3. Inspect `build/gz-doxygen.warn`

The targeted warnings above no longer appear in the regenerated warning log:
- the total `@param mismatch or stray @param` count drops from 181 to 131
- the remaining warnings in this category are all `Q_DECLARE_METATYPE(ComponentTypeId)` spillover warnings

### Scope

This PR intentionally fixes only the first 50 live warnings from the `@param mismatch or stray @param` category.

It does not address:
- the remaining 131 `Q_DECLARE_METATYPE(ComponentTypeId)` spillover warnings
- the 310 recursive `gz::physics` relation warnings
- the obsolete Doxygen configuration-tag warnings printed during doc generation

r600: rescope C-Track after 4-way rubber-duck consensus

C0 (GPR spill path) added as P0 correctness blocker — all 4 reviews agreed
spill fallback outranks scheduler refinements. C-IF (Mandate #13 bcsel cap)
added as P1 correctness guard. C4+C5 merged into one interleaved pass per
mandate #8. C5 weighted-sum replaced with lexicographic/gated priority
(PV/PS affinity > clause continuity > critical path > Sarkar > channel
diversity). C2 deferred unless C4+C5 reveals missing state. C3 becomes
verify/close against existing SFN barrier mechanisms. C6 already CLOSED.

Key findings adopted:
- 5-step cascade = legality (must always run), clause continuity = heuristic
  (preference among legal candidates) — different layers, don't conflate
- Topology rejection (84 events) >> physical rejection (6 events) — channel
  geometry is the dominant loss mode, not silicon legality
- CPU bottleneck (GPU 62-75% idle) means scheduler changes must be lightweight
- required_instr is inter-group only — insufficient for intra-group barriers
- Existing SFN mechanisms (AluGroup, readport validation, KCACHE reservation,

Remove FprimeRouter Copy (#4984)

* Remove unnecessary buffer allocation/copy in FprimeRouter for file and unknown packets

Instead of allocating a new buffer and copying data for file and unknown
packet types, pass the original buffer directly to the receiver. The
buffer is not returned to the deframer via dataReturnOut until it comes
back on fileBufferReturnIn, at which point an empty context is
constructed for the return.

This eliminates the need for bufferAllocate and bufferDeallocate ports,
as well as the AllocationReason enum and AllocationError event.

fix(maglev): remove all borrow_mut cache clears that cause use-after-free

The maglev_cache.borrow_mut() = None pattern munmaps JIT code pages
while parent recursive frames still hold return addresses into them.
This causes either RefCell borrow panics (debug) or use-after-free
returning wrong results (release mutual recursion tests).

Fix: only set the deopt flag (mark_jit_maglev_deopted). The flag
prevents future JIT execution. The cache stays alive until the
BytecodeArray is dropped, ensuring all parent frames remain valid.

Removed 3 instances across call_js_function, finish_direct_call,
and finish_direct_call_r15 code paths.

commit.message:
r600: close C6 Post-RA PV/PS hazard — not real in SFN architecture

SFN RA spills insert at block boundaries only (sfn_ra.cpp:500-560).
PV/PS chains are intra-block. Therefore RA spills cannot break PV/PS
chains, and the C6 Post-RA Repair pass is unnecessary.

KCACHE rollback fixes for the Active PV/PS Seeker committed on x130e
as a0ef9ca434b (amended into the seeker commit).

Debugging revealed the disk cache (Phase 4D, f579f0d42a9) was silently
serving pre-compiled bytecode, hiding the fact that SFN changes were
never being exercised. Always nuke ~/.cache/mesa_shader_cache* and set
MESA_SHADER_CACHE_DISABLE=true when validating compiler changes.

fix: msal-react passes undefined to ssoSilent when no request provided (#8520)

When `useMsalAuthentication` is used with `InteractionType.Silent` and
no `authenticationRequest`, `instance.ssoSilent(undefined)` was called,
causing the silent flow to fail (issue #8382).

## Changes

- **`useMsalAuthentication`**: Fall back to `{}` instead of `undefined`
when no `loginRequest` is available, so `ssoSilent` always receives a
valid request object
- **`MsalAuthenticationTemplate.spec.tsx`**: Updated test expectation to
match the new `{}` fallback
- **`StandardController`**: Removed redundant `prompt` assignment from
`validRequest` construction (already covered by the spread)
- **`hooks.md`**: Updated Silent interaction type note — request is now
optional (empty object used as fallback), though providing `loginHint`,
`sid`, or `account` is still strongly recommended

```ts
// Before
return instance.ssoSilent(loginRequest as SsoSilentRequest); // loginRequest could be undefined

// After
return instance.ssoSilent(loginRequest ? (loginRequest as SsoSilentRequest) : {});
```

---------
